name: section_controls
label: Section Controls
help_text: This group contains inputs which affect various parts of this section such as device visibility, spacing, color override, or other miscellaneous settings related to the entire section.

children.name: anchor_id
children.label: Anchor ID
children.help_text: Adding an anchor to this section will allow you to link to this section from this or other pages. Anchor links which point to a section on the current page will scroll the user to that section while anchors links to a different page will load the page with the user already scrolled to it.
children.inline_help_text: Add a unique ID to allow anchor links to this section. Only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores are permitted and this must begin with a letter. (Example: section-name) When linking to this section in an inline hyperlink, place a hashtag before the anchor ID.

children.name: lazy_load_images
children.label: Lazy Load Images
children.help_text: Lazy loading images can help improve page load times by telling the browser to load certain images last. This feature is only supported by modern browsers and any unsupported browsers will load the images as normal.
children.inline_help_text: Enable this to lazy load imagery to defer loading them initially. Depending on the section and how things are built, this input may not necessarily affect all images in this section. WARNING: This input should only be enabled for sections which appear below the fold. Not supported by all browsers.

children.name: device_visibility
children.label: Device Visibility
children.inline_help_text: Use these inputs to show/hide this section for certain device sizes. When only showing this section on certain screen sizes, ensure this section does not contain necessary features for the page's functionality or design.

children.name: spacing
children.label: Spacing
children.inline_help_text: Use these inputs to configure the top and bottom spacing of your section. This can be useful to increase or decrease the height of a section, reduce spacing between sections which share the same background color, or scale this spacing based on screen size when using certain types of backgrounds.

children.name: top_padding_desktop_custom_measurement_unit
children.label: Top Padding: Custom Padding Measurement Unit (Desktop)
children.inline_help_text: This input determines the significance of the Top Padding: Custom Padding Amount input. This can be set to a static number of pixels, a percentage based on screen width, or a percentage based on screen height.

children.name: top_padding_laptop_custom_measurement_unit
children.label: Top Padding: Custom Padding Measurement Unit (Laptop)
children.help_text: This is useful when the background of this section requires a specific amount of space that should either be a static amount of space or scale based on screen width or height.

children.name: top_padding_tablet_custom_measurement_unit
children.label: Top Padding: Custom Padding Measurement Unit (Tablet)
children.inline_help_text: This input determines the significance of the Top Padding: Custom Padding Amount (Tablet) input. This can be set to a static number of pixels, a percentage based on screen width, or a percentage based on screen height.

children.name: top_padding_mobile_custom_measurement_unit
children.label: Top Padding: Custom Padding Measurement Unit (Mobile)

children.name: bottom_padding_desktop_custom_measurement_unit
children.label: Bottom Padding: Custom Padding Measurement Unit (Desktop)
children.help_text: This is useful when the background of this section requires a specific amount of space that should either be a static amount of space or scale based on screen width or height.

children.name: bottom_padding_laptop_custom_measurement_unit
children.label: Bottom Padding: Custom Padding Measurement Unit (Laptop)

children.name: bottom_padding_tablet_custom_measurement_unit
children.label: Bottom Padding: Custom Padding Measurement Unit (Tablet)
children.help_text: This is useful when the background of this section requires a specific amount of space that should either be a static amount of space or scale based on screen width or height.

children.name: bottom_padding_mobile_custom
children.label: Bottom Padding: Custom Padding Amount (Mobile)
children.inline_help_text: The significance of this number is determined by the Bottom Padding: Custom Padding Measurement Unit (Mobile) input.

children.name: bottom_padding_mobile_custom_measurement_unit
children.label: Bottom Padding: Custom Padding Measurement Unit (Mobile)

children.name: text_color_override
children.label: Text Color Override
children.inline_help_text: These inputs override the color of any text which appears directly on your background. Use these to make all text use a light color when using a darker background to ensure the text is legible. NOTE: Elements other than text may be affected as well and some text may not necessarily be white.

children.name: override_on_all_screen_sizes
children.label: White Text on All Screen Sizes

name: background
label: Background Layers
help_text: Using multiple layers allows you to stack levels of imagery on top of each other such as a background image with an overlay or texture. Depending on the imagery, you might consider using the Device Visibility controls to display a different set of backgrounds for certain screen sizes.
inline_help_text: Add one or more background layers here. Layers are overlaid on top of each other with the first layer in your list being the back and the last layer in your list being the front. After changing settings in this module group, click Apply to preview your changes.

children.name: device_visibility
children.label: Device Visibility
children.inline_help_text: Use these inputs to show/hide this background layer for certain device sizes. When only showing this layer on certain screen sizes, ensure this layer is not necessary for your content to be legible.

children.name: swoop_custom_color
children.label: Swoop: Custom Color
children.inline_help_text: The opacity for this module is intended to be 100%, otherwise your color will display lighter than intended.

children.name: background_opacity
children.label: Background: Opacity
children.inline_help_text: Use this module to adjust this background layer's opacity (transparency). 100 is solid and 0 is completely transparent.

children.name: background_custom_color
children.label: Background: Custom Color
children.inline_help_text: The opacity for this module is intended to be 100%, otherwise your color will display lighter than intended.

children.name: background_gradient_custom_start_color
children.label: Background: Gradient Custom Start Color
children.help_text: The opacity for this module is intended to be 100%, otherwise your color will display lighter than intended.

children.name: background_gradient_start_color_position
children.label: Background Gradient: Start Color Position
children.help_text: This number represents a percentage of the linear gradient from start to finish, with 0 being the start and 100 being the end. Anything between this number and 0 will use your gradient start color, whereas anything between this number and the end color will be the transition between the colors.
children.inline_help_text: This number determines where the start color will be positioned in the gradient. This number must be less than the value of your gradient end color, otherwise it may cause issues with the direction of your gradient. (Default: 0)

children.name: background_gradient_custom_end_color
children.label: Background: Gradient Custom End Color
children.help_text: The opacity for this module is intended to be 100%, otherwise your color will display lighter than intended.

children.name: background_gradient_end_color_position
children.label: Background Gradient: End Color Position
children.help_text: This number represents a percentage of the linear gradient from start to finish, with 0 being the start and 100 being the end. Anything between this number and 100 will use your gradient end color, whereas anything between this number and the start color will be the transition between the colors.
children.inline_help_text: This number determines where the end color will be positioned in the gradient. This number must be greater than the value of your gradient start color, otherwise it may cause issues with the direction of your gradient. (Default: 100)

children.name: background_image_size
children.label: Background: Image Size
choices: Native Size (use image's native height and width)

children.name: background_image_parallax_fixed_when_scrolling
children.label: Background: Image Parallax / Fixed When Scrolling
children.help_text: When using this effect, pick your image carefully or use an overlay when needed since since the positioning of the image in relation to your content will be highly dependent on screen size and scroll distance.
children.inline_help_text: Enable this to make your image fixed so it does not scroll with the rest of the page (similar to a parallax effect but unlike a parallax the image will not move at all). This is useful when you need to use one background image across consecutive sections or your section changes height during use.

children.name: background_fallback_background_image
children.label: Background: Fallback Background Image
children.help_text: This image will show while the video is loading or on devices which cannot display the video.
children.inline_help_text: IMPORTANT: The height and width attributes of this image must match the size or aspect ratio of the video, otherwise your video may not be positioned properly. For best appearance, this image should usually be identical to the first frame of your video.

children.name: background_offset
children.label: Background: Offset
children.help_text: These inputs determine how much of the height and width of the section this background should cover. This is useful for when you only want a background layer to cover a certain percentage of the section. If your layer should cover the entire height and width of the section, the value of each of these inputs should be 0.

children.name: background_left_offset_percentage
children.label: Background: Left Offset Percentage
children.help_text: This number determines how much of the width of the section this background should cover. This number represents a percentage of the section's width, with 0 being the left edge and 100 being the right edge. Use this input to control the offset from the left edge of the section or set it to 0 if the background should go all the way to the left edge. This number must be less than 100 minus the value of your Right Offset Percentage value, otherwise your background may not display properly. (Default: 0)

children.name: background_right_offset_percentage
children.label: Background: Right Offset Percentage
children.help_text: This number determines how much of the width of the section this background should cover. This number represents a percentage of the section's width, with 0 being the right edge and 100 being the left edge. Use this input to control the offset from the right edge of the section or set it to 0 if the background should go all the way to the left edge. This number must be less than 100 minus the value of your Left Offset Percentage value, otherwise your background may not display properly. (Default: 0)

children.name: background_top_offset_percentage
children.label: Background: Top Offset Percentage
children.help_text: This number determines how much of the height of the section this background should cover. This number represents a percentage of the section's height, with 0 being the top edge and 100 being the bottom edge. Use this input to control the offset from the top edge of the section or set it to 0 if the background should go all the way to the top edge. This number must be less than 100 minus the value of your Bottom Offset Percentage value, otherwise your background may not display properly. (Default: 0)

children.name: background_bottom_offset_percentage
children.label: Background: Bottom Offset Percentage
children.help_text: This number determines how much of the height of the section this background should cover. This number represents a percentage of the section's height, with 0 being the bottom edge and 100 being the top edge. Use this input to control the offset from the bottom edge of the section or set it to 0 if the background should go all the way to the bottom edge. This number must be less than 100 minus the value of your Top Offset Percentage value, otherwise your background may not display properly. (Default: 0)

name: layout_controls
label: Layout Controls
help_text: This group contains inputs which are unique to this section for controlling the overall layout or style. Depending on the section, some inputs outside of this section may change based on the configuration here.

children.name: columns_image_type
children.label: Columns: Image Type
choices: Column-Width and Forced Aspect Ratio Image (Best for Photos)

children.name: show_border_divider_between_columns
children.label: Columns: Show Border Divider Between Columns

name: content
label: Content
default: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla non tempor massa. Nunc magna purus, dictum vel mattis vel, viverra sit amet justo.

children.name: image
children.label: Image
children.help_text: For best appearance, the height of the image in each column should be roughly the same.

children.name: description
children.label: Description
children.default: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla non tempor massa. Nunc magna purus, dictum vel mattis vel, viverra sit amet justo.

children.name: cta_title_attribute
children.label: CTA: Title Attribute
children.inline_help_text: CTA title attribute gives additional information about the page being linked to. Leave this blank to use your button text.

children.name: cta_section_anchor_id
children.label: CTA: Section Anchor ID
children.help_text: Add the anchor ID of a section on this page to link to it. To link to a section on a different page, use the Custom Link option and add the section ID to the end of the URL preceded by a hashtag.

default.description: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla non tempor massa. Nunc magna purus, dictum vel mattis vel, viverra sit amet justo.

children.name: cta_title_attribute
children.label: CTA: Title Attribute
children.inline_help_text: CTA title attribute gives additional information about the page being linked to. Leave this blank to use your button text.

children.name: cta_section_anchor_id
children.label: CTA: Section Anchor ID
children.help_text: Add the anchor ID of a section on this page to link to it. To link to a section on a different page, use the Custom Link option and add the section ID to the end of the URL preceded by a hashtag.
